Read Canopy Height Data (OzTreeMap)

Source: R/read_canopy_height.R
read_canopy_height.Rd

Wrapper around read_tern() for Canopy Height Models from OzTreeMap (TERN/36c98155). Provides an Australia-wide composite at 30 m resolution (static product).

Important: The coordinate reference system is EPSG:3577 (GDA94/Australian Albers, projected in metres). If you are extracting values at point locations from geographic coordinates (latitude/longitude), you must first reproject your points to EPSG:3577 using terra::project() or sf::st_transform().

Usage

read_canopy_height(api_key = NULL, max_tries = 3L, initial_delay = 1L)

Arguments

api_key

A character string containing your TERN API key. Defaults to automatic detection from your .Renviron or .Rprofile. See get_key() for setup.

max_tries

An integer giving the maximum number of download retries before an error is raised. Defaults to 3.

initial_delay

An integer giving the initial retry delay in seconds (doubles with each attempt). Defaults to 1.

Value

A terra::rast() object (single layer, EPSG:3577).

References

TERN portal: https://portal.tern.org.au/metadata/TERN/36c98155

OzTreeMap: https://www.tern.org.au/landscapes/

See also

read_tern(), read_soil_diversity()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # interactive()
# Read canopy height for all of Australia
r <- read_canopy_height()
plot(r)

# Extract at a location (Murray Bridge, SA) -- note: must reproject first
pt <- terra::vect(
  matrix(c(138.6, -34.9), ncol = 2),
  type = "points",
  crs = "EPSG:4326"  # geographic (lat/lon)
)
pt_proj <- terra::project(pt, "EPSG:3577")  # project to raster CRS
val <- terra::extract(r, pt_proj)
print(val)
}

On this page
